evidence: Ray's advice—'If you only have one, don't expect to make any money'—reveals that acquisition value is created primarily by competition, not by the intrinsic value of what's being sold.
Ray's advice based on his recent exit: 'Started with 20-25 prospects, ended with 1 acquirer. If you only have one, don't expect to make any money.'
Ray's experience shows acquirers can manipulate success metrics, making earnouts a one-sided bet. His experience: Could have made up to $1M, walked away with $50-100K due to earnout structure.
Strategy derived from Ray: Optimize for salary + bonuses (contractually guaranteed) rather than earnouts.
Ilse was building to a higher standard than needed (30-day stories) until V clarified that acquirer demos have lower requirements. Gap between engineer assumptions and actual business needs.
Logan knows Kathy Pham, VP and head of AI at Workday. Plan: Email her saying 'we're in acquisition conversations'.
Clara assessed as acqui-hire opportunity. AI recruiter company, California-based, raised 'a couple mil'. Ball is in Careerspan's court to show scan output.

source_display: Better at Job = Worse at Interviewing Paradox
evidence: V: 'The better you are at a job, the worse you are at interviewing because you've been fucking employed, because people have not wanted to let you go. They wanted you to be on their team.'
V: 'You're essentially reallocating a lot of low grade assets that are filtering and vetting your candidates to maybe a smaller number of higher grade employees that are more so engaging...'

Demo showed 'massive jump in score' when stories were added. 'A candidate with 6 rich stories beats a candidate with 20 years of undifferentiated experience.' Coaching on story development has 10x ROI.
Building AI talent sourcing operation focused on India-based AI engineers. Target market: $700B. Recently returned from Saudi Arabia business trek, made connections at Aramco Ventures.
Technical counterpart to Shivam. Raised implementation questions V deferred to head of AI. Will map out workflow and integration requirements.
Building talent pipeline connecting Indian AI engineers with US companies. Have ~100 AI engineering resumes/profiles already. Goal: candidate intelligence graph.
Discussion about employers banning interview recording. V noted Granola uses kernel-level access (undetectable). Both expressed frustration with employer power imbalance in hiring.
